name: Damaru Drum
description: The Damaru is a small, two-headed drum, shaped like an hourglass, that is closely associated with the Hindu deity Shiva. It represents the primal sound of creation, the Nada Brahma, from which all of existence manifested. The drum is typically made of wood, metal, or skull, with leather drumheads stretched across either end. These heads are struck by knotted cords or beads attached to the drum, which swing back and forth when the drum is twirled. Shiva uses the Damaru to create the rhythm of the universe, controlling the cycles of creation, preservation, and destruction. The sound produced is believed to be a powerful force, capable of both generating and dissolving reality. Beyond Shiva, the Damaru is also associated with other deities and spiritual practices, often used in rituals and ceremonies to invoke divine energy and promote healing. The drum's rhythmic beats are said to awaken the inner self and facilitate spiritual transformation, connecting the individual to the cosmic vibrations. Its presence signifies the dynamism and constant flux of the universe, reminding devotees of the impermanence of all things and the eternal cycle of existence. The Damaru serves as a potent symbol of Shiva's power and the creative force that sustains the cosmos.
subtitle: The primal sound of creation.
origin: Created by Shiva as the sound of the universe.
currentLocation: Held by Shiva and represented in temples worldwide.

keyMyths.title: Sound of Creation
keyMyths.description: The Damaru's sound is the first sound of creation, Nada Brahma, from which the universe emanated. It represents the vibration that sustains all of existence and Shiva’s divine energy.

keyMyths.title: Shiva's Dance
keyMyths.description: When Shiva dances as Nataraja, the Lord of Dance, the Damaru beats the rhythm of the universe, driving the cosmic dance of creation and destruction. The drum's rhythm guides the flow of energy.

sources.author: Stella Kramrisch
sources.description: Discusses the symbolism and significance of Shiva and his attributes, including the Damaru.
sources.text: Discusses the symbolism and significance of Shiva and his attributes, including the Damaru.

sources.author: Alain Danielou
sources.description: Explores the multifaceted aspects of Hindu deities and their associated symbols and instruments.
